name: Anthony Braxton
abstract: Anthony Braxton (born June 4, 1945) is an American experimental composer, educator, music theorist, improviser, and multi-instrumentalist who is best known for playing saxophones, particularly the alto sax. He grew up on the South Side of Chicago and was a key early member of the Association for the Advancement of Creative Musicians.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: George Coleman Octet/ Ted Curson Sextet/ Anthony Braxton Quintet

about_quotes.speaker: Ran Blake
about_quotes.evidence: when I did “Yardbird Suite” that was a few years ago with Anthony... We did it in an album called “One Night in Vienna.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Ran Blake interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 16, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Marilyn Crispell
about_quotes.evidence: I remember one particular instance of playing a concert with my whole heart... I was playing with Braxton and everything
about_quotes.doc_title: Marilyn Crispell interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 21, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: Braxton came in... He came in about a year later, and we turned it into a quartet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: When you want to talk about avant-garde, you’re talking about; Albert Ayler, Pharoah Saunders, or one phase of Archie Shepp, and Ornette Coleman, Don Cherry, Anthony Braxton, or people like that, the Chicago Art Ensemble, Joseph Jarman, and those people.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: One time I was invited up to someone’s apartment with Chick, Dave Holland, Barry Altschul, and Anthony Braxton. I think Anthony and I were auditioning to play with the group called “Circle”
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: But then I left the next year and then that’s when Roscoe Mitchell and I think Joseph Jarman, Anthony Braxton I think, yeah. That’s when it was officially formed, the AACM.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025
